name: lora-dataset-creation
whenToUse: Turn task traces, teacher outputs, or corrected answers into a clean SFT / preference dataset for LoRA/QLoRA training or distillation.
workflow: Collect raw traces (agent runs, teacher answers, corrections) with their verifier verdicts.
Filter to verified-good examples only; route rejected ones to a rejected set.
Normalize to the messages schema (system/user/assistant) with metadata + provenance.
Build preference pairs (chosen=verified, rejected=failed) where both exist for a prompt.
De-leak against eval holdouts; split train/holdout with stable hashing.
Validate row schema and emit SFT jsonl + DPO jsonl + a dataset card.
verification: Every kept example passed its verifier (score_case / gate / unit test).
No training row overlaps an eval holdout (leakage check passes).
All rows conform to the messages schema; preference pairs have distinct chosen/rejected.
commonFailures: Training on unverified or teacher-hallucinated answers.
Leaking eval/holdout prompts into the training split.
Losing provenance so bad data can't be traced and removed.

examples.output: training/sft.jsonl (412 rows, all verified), training/dpo.jsonl (88 pairs), rejected.jsonl (130); leakageChecked: true.
